entry.title: Re: SAC Compass - any instructions available?
entry.content: Yes you can do that under title of the page

entry.title: MDG functionality has a navigation issue when clicking on any change request
entry.content:
Hi Experts, in MDG S4,it is observed that in MDG functionality has a navigation issue when clicking on any change request, it opens Business partner search page , whereas it should give a detailed view of Change request. Please suggest the solution for this.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: RAP Unmanaged Scenario - Creation of RAP BO does not...
entry.content: Hi, just for clarification - I was the user that opened the SAP note, so just to confirm - this was a program error, and continues to be a program error in I_EquipmentTP. SAP themselves, as mentioned in the note suggest to use A_Equipment, which is unreleased for cloud RAP Object - actually the RAP object standing behind API_Equipment Web API to process equipments. I would like to leave this for other developers that found the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Modify Scope in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi Jwangman,

In the copy you have created, make sure you select the appropriate Process Hierarchy Nodes in the Relations tab. Make sure also that the copy is published.

Then, in the scope of the project, make sure you select Custom also in the Solution Scenarios.

By doing this, I was able to select the custom process (copy of standard) in the project.

Let me know if you need further details.

entry.title: Difference between “Assigned Restriction Types” and “Permission Filters” for Key Figure Authorizatio
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a question about setting permissions in SAP IBP:
I want to ensure that only the key figure “SAP REP QTY” is editable, while all other key figures are read-only.

I’ve noticed there are two options for this:

  1. Assigned Restriction Types in the “Maintain Business Roles” app:

    • Here, I can specify which key figures should be editable within a certain section.

       

  2. Permission Filters:

    • Alternatively, I can create a permission filter in which I define exactly which key figures are editable or non-editable.

My questions are:

  • How do these two settings work together?
  • When is it sufficient to use only “Assigned Restriction Types,” and when should I also define a “Permission Filter”?
  • Why do both options exist, and in which scenarios is each method typically used?

I appreciate any help, tips, or best practices on how to implement this cleanly. Thank you in advance!

Best regards,
Oliver

entry.title: Re: SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Subscription with BYOD plan
entry.content:
Great Blog !!!

Just few Questions:

1. What are the additional Benefits of BYOD over the current 'PaPM Run-Time DB' ?

2. Under a same tenant, can i have both 'PaPM Runtime DB' and 'BYOD' subscriptions?

3. Is there is any migration path available, to move existing developments from  'PaPM Run-Time DB' to BYOD ?

entry.title: Re: Off Cycle Increases through Performance-Compensation integrated worksheets
entry.content: It looks really nice, I ve set in in our demo and works perfectly. Just one thing - is it possible to set budget eg. for manager for whole year? If they use it for one employee and then launch form for second employee, there will be increased budget... I think, that it is not possible, but I want to ask 🙂 thanks, Monika

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a str
entry.content:
Personally think this architectural decisions at SAP are horrendous. 

Now a lot of customers have to buy two solutions and its super confusing as we see here in the comment section. Licensing nightmare as usual with SAP.

Also customers who have been sold integration suite as the strategic integration platform for B2G are now being screwed over with the need to get another cloud subscription.  

Its not only DRC, SAP is doing this with other products as well.

entry.title: Re: Modify Scope in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello Guru,

in order to add custom processes in your project, you have to add the scenario "Custom" in your scope. Please open the "Manage Scopes" Application. Then Select your existing Scope. Click on edit and add a further scenario called "Custom". Now you will be able to view your custom processes in your existing scope. 
Hope this helps.

Best regards,
Maya

entry.title: Re: Automatic E-Mail Alert when Exception in EWM MFS is fired
entry.content: If you are using SAP MFS you need the EWM Advanced Licence.  Due to my knowledge no separate license needed

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: hi Dan, i found the issue where i'm going wrong, in the code i'm not passing it as inputs.input1 and also i didn't define the previous jsnode output as object .Thank you very much for the solution.

entry.title: Re: Are there any other transaction codes that have the s...
entry.content:
Hello @ThakurRavinderSingh 

The /SCWM/PRDVC transaction is a variant transaction that calls SM30 with the /SCWM/V_PRDV_C view:

 

The view links the /SCWM/TPRDV_CAT and /SCWM/TPRDV_CATT tables:

 

Both are of "application table" delivery class:

Therefore changes in /SCWM/PRDVC are not transportable.

 

Additionally the maintenance view generated for /SCWM/V_PRDV_C has the recording routine disabled:

 

I think you can safely remove the S_TRANSPRT authorization from the profile as transports are not required for /SCWM/PRDVC.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: New configuration Settlement Management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Daniel,

After settlement, report WB2R_BVDETAIL will show  you the link between the rebate credit memo and the invoices which were used in the calculation of the rebate. This is the detailed statement of the settlement.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Automatic E-Mail Alert when Exception in EWM MFS is fired
entry.content:
Hi!

I'm not using MFS, I just need to trigger the email alert.

 

Thanks

Ksm

entry.title: Re: SAP PAPM Cloud SM integration with SAP cTMS
entry.content:
Really appreciate the effort on bring "Integration of SAP PaPM Cloud SM and SAP Cloud Transport Management (SAP cTMS)" feature , which is really very much essential from Audit and Traceability Purpose.

Does team have any plan to brining "integration with SAP cTMS" for SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model as well?

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: My pleasure 🙂

entry.title: Re: Inquiry regarding multiplex storage configuration
entry.content:
I share here answer provided by SAP IQ Support :

Basically SAP doesn't certify / test with the storage for a specific vendor.
If Veritas infoscale Storage works as same as NFS or GPFS on Linux,
IQ multiplex will also be able to work with the Veritas Storage.

I checked in past cases, but there is no customer case that IQ multiplex uses 
on Veritas infoscale Storage.
For Veritas Cluster Service, the below KBA exists:

2447953 - Is the Veritas Cluster Service required to manage IQ Multiplex? SAP IQ
3143339 - Install SYBASE IQ in multiplex in Unix cluster

entry.title: Re: Cloud identity service SCIM api to retrieve personal...
entry.content:
A usual business user does not have the authorization to invoke IAS APIs.
The personal data of a user is 'exposed' via the user profile page. Simply invoke the IAS tenant URL (e.g. https://xxxxx.accounts.ondemand) and then you end up on your user profile (after successful authentication).
In the top right corner of the profile page there is a button 'View My Data' and you'll get all your user profile data displayed.

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – January 2025 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Welcome to the first SAP Build Code release communications blog post of the new year! We're excited to kick off 2025 with many great new features and enhancements designed to streamline your development experience.

Dive into our new Project Overview providing a streamlined and comprehensive view on your projects. We have also made several improvements to simplify your work with Remote Function Calls (RFC). Another notable addition is the inline editing capability for CDS entities, enabling you to add or edit properties directly in the CDS Graphical Modeler. In addition to these exciting new features, we have also curated a collection of informative blog posts that delve into various aspects related to SAP Build Code.

To explore these features in depth and stay ahead of the curve, check out the details of what's new!

 

The new Project Overview

In our latest release of SAP Build Code, we've introduced an exciting change to the default left pane view. We've switched from the Project Explorer to the new Project Overview. This new Project Overview layout aligns more closely with the Storyboard aesthetics and not only includes all the current functionalities of the Project Explorer but also offers additional capabilities.

As you can see in the screenshot below, the Project Overview presents the following sections: Data Models, Services, Analytical Models, User Interface, and External Resources.

Sections of the Project Overview

You can perform project level actions from the context menu of the project, create new objects of each type from the section ‘+’ action, and perform additional actions from the context menu on each object.


Performing Actions in the Project Overview

Moreover, we are thrilled to announce that in the future the Project Overview will support not just projects created via the SAP Build Code Lobby or those using the "full-stack with productivity tools" template, but it will also support full-stack applications built on SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) Java or CAP Node.js. Stay tuned for these upcoming enhancements, and we hope you enjoy the improved interface!

 

Improvements when Working with RFC

When you are working with Remote Function Calls (RFCs) in your Full-Stack Application Using Productivity Tools projects you should try out the following enhancement: When adding a Remote Function Call to a Full-Stack Application Using Productivity Tools project, the run configuration for the external resource is now updated automatically. You can use the run configuration to select which destination to use for RFC when previewing the full-stack application.

For Remote Function Calls, both basic authentication and now also principal propagation are supported, allowing you to explore services in the Service Center, preview apps via run configuration, and run deployed apps seamlessly without the hassle of entering user credentials. For further information, see Explore SAP System Functions.


Run Configuration: Functions (RFC)

 

Inline Editing for CDS Entities

We are thrilled to announce the launch of our new inline editing feature in the CDS Graphical Modeler! This enhancement allows users to add or edit properties directly within an entity in the diagram shown in the CDS Graphical Modeler. This means fewer clicks, less navigating and a more intuitive experience. This addition complements the existing property sheet functionality and will make your work smoother and faster.


Adding a new property

 

Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE

We'd like to inform you of important updates affecting SAP Web IDE: SAP Web IDE sunset will take place as part of the sunset of the SAP BTP, Neo environment by December 31, 2028. As a successor of SAP Web IDE, SAP introduced the SAP Business Application Studio five years ago, which has been serving as a robust and modernized alternative for development needs.

Furthermore, we'd like address the recent announcement regarding the deprecation of the SAP Java Buildpack 1. This component is set to be deprecated from the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment by June 2025. Consequently, Java tools currently supported in SAP Web IDE will also be phased out by the same date in June 2025.

For a more detailed examination of these changes, how it may affect your development process, and guidance on migrating to the recommended environments, please read Liat Borenstein’s comprehensive article: Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE.

 

Further readings

  • Overview of GenAI in SAP Build and SAP Build Code 

Since its initial release in March 2024, SAP Build Code has rapidly evolved to bring cutting-edge generative AI (GenAI) functionalities to developers across diverse use cases. The latest blog post of my colleagues David Bizer and Marc Huber delves into the current features of SAP Build Code and the wide array of use cases they support. They also provide recommendations tailored to specific functionalities to ensure you get the most out of this powerful tool.

If you are interested in an overview on generative AI in SAP Build, you shouldn’t miss this blog post written by my colleague Marc Huber: AI in SAP Build - Overview and Best Practices.

  • Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation

Streamline your development process by using the new feature for multi-agent support for advanced CAP application generation with SAP Build Code Joule. The key: harness specialized agents for various tasks using just a single prompt. In his latest blog post, Xin Ye explains this new feature in detail by guiding through an end-to-end flow.

  • Migrating SAPUI5 Applications to TypeScript with the Help of Joule 

As you probably know, Joule supports you with writing freestyle SAPUI5 code in SAP Build Code. Joule's latest feature offers an AI-driven migration of UI5 applications to TypeScript, which, compared to JavaScript, enables an improved development efficiency. This blog post, written by Martin Haeuser is dedicated to providing insights into the latest feature that offers AI-driven migration of SAPUI5 applications to TypeScript.

  • Differences between Extension Projects and Adaptation Projects in SAPUI5 

Looking to enhance your SAPUI5 applications? The blog post "Extending SAPUI5 apps: Adaptation projects vs. Extension projects" written by my colleague Oliver Graeff, explores the flexibility of SAPUI5, which allows not just developers but also key users and end users to extend standard apps. Discover how developers harness the power of adaptation projects in SAP Business Application Studio, the development environment of SAP Build Code. While adaptation projects offer advanced, strategic solutions for code-based extensions, there are also the predecessor extension projects. To address frequent confusions, Oliver explains the differences between the two of them.

  • Deprecation of the karma-ui5 Plugin 

SAPUI5 provides many testing options, like unit and integration tests, which are summarized in UI5 Testing. As the Karma test runner is deprecated since April 2023, the UI5 team now also has to deprecate karma-ui5. For further details, please read the following blog post from Oliver Graeff:  Deprecation of karma-ui5 plugin.

  • SAP Fiori tools 2501 Update 

SAP Fiori tools is consistently evolving, bringing innovative features that empower both experienced developers and casual users. The recent updates focus on harnessing AI, simplifying the user interface for adaptations, and enhancing the overall usability of the tools. Read this blog post written by Christoph Gollmick and discover how these new enhancements can elevate your SAP Fiori development journey.

  • SAP HANA SQL Notebooks in SAP Build, SAP Business Application Studio and Visual Studio Code

With SQL Notebooks you can incorporate SQL statements into your projects. It offers effortless access to your underlying SAP HANA database or HDI Container but also includes scripts for setup, adding test data, and delivering educational functions. In this blog post, Volker Saggau provides a comprehensive overview of SAP HANA SQL Notebooks in various environments such as SAP Build, SAP Business Application Studio, and Visual Studio Code.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: Re: Disabling the Mobile App for Multiple Profiles
entry.content:
Hi naveenkumar22,

thank for your reply.

So this feature can not be controled from Admin Center in he SuccessFactors?

entry.title: Re: AZT_TM_adhoc Report deprecation date confirmation
entry.content:
In the past SAP has announced to deprecate the legacy reporting tools (table reports, canvas, tiles). While tile reports and dashboards will end with H2 2025, the deprecation announcement for table reports have been withdrawn. Please details here:

Deprecation of Table Reports | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: How to get the username in error 06 307 Info record &...
entry.content:
Hello @biluka_evcwnbrg 

The 06307 is not and can't be customizable. It tells that there is a locking conflict when accessing a purchasing info record in the change mode.

Unfortunately, SAP has implemented the message that it doesn't show a blocking user id. That can't be changed without modifying SAP standard code.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: How to construct cXML for Ariba in SAP Process Automation
entry.content:
We need to post a commerce XML with the data extracted to Ariba Solutions.  Is there any elegant way to do this and has it been done before.  I used a simple string concatenation and used the string for the body and it is a pain to give escape characters.

return {
    'method' : 'POST',
    'headers': {'Accept' : '*/*',
                'Accept-Encoding' : 'gzip, deflate, br',
                'Connection': 'keep-alive',
                'Content-Type' : 'multipart/related;type="application/xml";start="<mimepart1>";boundary="----=_Part_XERRR_XDDFFDDFF"'
               },
    'url'    : url,
    'body'   : cxmlString,
    'responseType' : 'text',
    'resolveBodyOnly' :true
};

entry.title: Re: Communicate from SAP Public Cloud to Java Application...
entry.content: The problem is currently jco.server.use_tls=0, this is actually not supported from ABAP cloud clients, they will always communicate via TLS, however your current JCo server expects plain WebSocket communication now with this setting. For testing purposes you can create and setup a simple p12 file containing an own server certificate which ABAP trusts.

entry.title: Re: The OData or EDMX specification file was not found at the destination
entry.content:
Hi Kiranmayee,

Have you resolved this issue? I have the same issue at the moment.

Thanks,
Jhani

entry.content:
Hi all, i created a RFC Destination in BTP cockpit, while checking the Connection ,it's showing as Connection Successful.

But this destination is not showing up in Build apps, under Integration Tab.
Not getting where i'm going wrong. Can anyone please help .
Thank you in Advance.

@Dan_Wroblewski  @DonWilliams @Sandra_Rossi @Ulrich_Schmidt

entry.title: HR Connectivity in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving landscape of digital business operations, compliance and data management are paramount. Recognizing the challenges organizations face, SAP has introduced two significant innovations in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, specifically targeting HR connectivity and data protection. In this blog post, I am excited to share deeper insights into how the latest innovations can help your organization.

 

Workforce: Assignment of Data Controllers

With data privacy regulations such as GDPR shaping business operations, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 streamlines compliance through an advanced data protection model. This release integrates privacy principles directly into the data management framework, ensuring that the handling of workforce data is in line with global data protection laws.

Key Features:

  • Integrated Data Controller Logic: By linking worker data to relevant data controllers, the innovation reduces manual effort in data assignment and ensures a robust data separation model. This integrated logic not only minimizes exposure to data-related risks but also streamlines compliance processes.
  • Access Restrictions: Ensures sensitive worker data is accessible only to authorized personnel, safeguarding personal information through role-based access controls.
  • Automatic Data Controller Assignments: Enhances operational efficiency by automatically assigning data controllers to workers, aligning with legal entities and simplifying joint controllership management.

The innovation is designed to empower organizations to effectively manage privacy risks by integrating advanced data protection measures directly into the cloud infrastructure. This strategic enhancement not only safeguards sensitive information but also significantly reduces the need for manual interventions regarding data controller assignments. By doing so, it ensures a smooth, seamless operation that upholds compliance standards with minimal effort, thereby increasing overall operational efficiency. This dual focus on risk mitigation and streamlined processes positions the innovation as a vital asset for organizations seeking to navigate the complexities of data privacy in today's digital landscape.

 

SAP Fiori App “Worker Overview”: Overview Tab

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 brings a new overview tab in the SAP Fiori “Worker Overview” app for HR professionals and managers. This tab serves as an intuitive and centralized dashboard, designed to present all critical worker data in one streamlined screen. 

Key Features:

  • Centralized Dashboard: Offers a holistic view of each worker by integrating contact details, employment agreements, organizational structure, and personnel data. This ensures that managers and HR professionals can make faster, more informed decisions.
  • Real-Time Data: Provides accuracy in decision-making by displaying up-to-date data, reducing the time wasted searching across multiple systems or screens.
  • Intuitive Navigation: SAP Fiori's clean design ensures that navigating through worker information is seamless, enhancing user experience across all roles.

 The latest innovation brings notable advancements in efficiency and communication, designed to optimize organizational workflows. With rapid access to comprehensive worker information, professionals can save valuable time and effort, empowering timely and well-informed decision-making. This heightened efficiency ensures that critical actions are based on accurate and up-to-date data. Additionally, streamlined access to manager and supplier details simplifies communication pathways, enhancing coordination both within the organization and with external partners. This dual focus on efficiency and communication not only strengthens internal operations but also fosters better collaboration across the board, promoting a more cohesive and agile organizational environment.

 

These innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 strongly position the platform as a leader in privacy by design, offering organizations the tools they need to stay compliant and operate efficiently in today's challenging data privacy environment. SAP continues to empower its users by refining business management processes and ensuring that data remains secure, accessible, and optimally managed. 

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

  

🔎Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: Announcing the New "In-Metro" Disaster Recovery Solution for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Yes, @gergo_timar this is currently within a given region and not (yet) inbetween regions:
"deploying services across multiple availability zones within a single region.."

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA for EHS-IM - Search for user retrieves us...
entry.content:
Dear Paulo,

the reason for this extra letter "D" in the username is used by EHS internally to handle different types of natural or legal persons. Whenever we store information about a person or organization, there are different objects which are used to represent this persons and/or organizations:

  • HR Persons (from HCM, see t-code PA20). For any of these, we add the letter "A" internally. As an example, an employee might have the number 2901, which will get stored internally in EHS as A00002901.
  • Applicants (from HCM). For any of these, we add the letter "B" internally.
  • External Parties (Business Partners), used for both, persons and organizations, we add the letter "C" internally. 
  • System Users (see t-code SU01D). For any of these, we add the letter "D" internally. If a user is picked as a responsible person at a location level with the "normal" username MFECKER, it's internally stored as DMFECKER.

Of course, these different objects can be connected, e.g. connecting the HR Person by infotype 0105/0001 with the system user. In such a case, we internally choose the most "useful" representation. Assuming you have a HR Person connected to a System User, it's obviously the HR Person. For an S/4HANA business user (which is the connected HR Person, System User, and Business Partner), it's the HR Person representation with some tweaks.

With this generic approach, we can store different kinds/types of people or organizations in a single field. Of course, in some places, we MUST look for a user - especially if there is a workflow in the system involved. This is the case for the "Responsible Persons" at a location level. These are used to be the responsible persons for the workflows for EHS Incident Management, EHS Chemical Data Management, and EHS Environment Management.

The issue with the error message "DUsername does not exist" is usually caused by a system copy. As an example, on a production system, there might be users which do not exist on a development box. After the system copy the users might get deleted on the copied instance, causing the problem.

In your search for responsible persons, the DUsernames should not show up - if these are not existing. First, I would ask you to check if the business users are correctly set up. Then, you might raise a support ticket to SAP.

With kind regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: HTTP Call working in ECC but not working in S4
entry.content: Hi @Sandra_Rossui

entry.title: Re: How to Implement "Navigate to List on Save" in a Fiori Elements App with OData V4
entry.content:
I couldn't find anything in the documentation for oData v4. You can try this as a workaround in your controllerExtensions for the object page. 

onAfterSave: function(mParameters) {
    this.base.getExtensionAPI().routing.navigateToRoute("list_view_name_here");
},

entry.title: Re: Career Site Builder Unified Data Model: New Job Search, Layout and Design
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,
We had created the new Job Location however this has now brought in two location fields in job requisition. Its not a very convenient thing for users who already have location_obj and now they have all new field to work upon . We had tried the integration center job as well which does not seems to be working either.
Has anyone got a chance to create a job to automate location_obj to jobLocation (New field) through integration center job . Any assistance would be really appreciated , because we do not want manual updating of the field.
Business rules not working as well the position to req mapping is not working for location_obj to jobLocation. SAP documentations are not great either on this

entry.title: Re: Modify Scope in SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Request for SAP Note/Reference on Penetration Testing for S/4HANA Public Cloud 2502
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

 

We are currently implementing the S/4HANA Public Cloud  version in our organization. Our management has requested to conduct penetration testing on our production system.

 

We have searched for publicly available information but could not find detailed guidance on the process. Could you please provide any SAP Note or reference documentation that outlines the detailed steps and technical parameters required for conducting penetration testing in this environment?

 

Looking forward to your support.

 

Regards,

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request for SAP Note/Reference on Penetration Testing...
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We don't cover this phase of testing separately with Test automation engine.

What we have is covered here.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/2d42264480fc472faaf802458f46807d.html

You can only run this test automate in the Test tenant.

Additionally you can refer :

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-cybersecurity-faqs-unravelled/ba-p/13556179

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Email Attachment Mail Adapter
entry.content:
According to the documentation at https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configure-mail-receiver-adapter, from what I understand you have two options, either add attachments provided in the body or the header by maintaining the list of attachments or add all attachments of the exchange by simply selecting the flag Add Message Attachments.

Your configuration won't work, you have chosen first option, then you need to add the attachment body in a header and specify the header in the list, you could add the attachment body in a script for instance.

I think, the second option i.e. the flag will do the trick for you because you added the attachment to the exchange in your script. So, remove the settings in the Attachments table and simply select the flag.

Alex

entry.title: Re: Adding a custom Picklist to a specific Time Type (Leave Request)
entry.content:
Hi Ashish,

Thank you for sharing this content very useful.

Let me ask you a question pls, If I want only one custom field to appear with a picklist for a specific time type that allows me to block half days of vacation for a specific country, and I only want it to appear for this time type and not for the remaining time types that also allow me to block half days vacation. its possible to do it ? since we cannot create a business rule to control the visibility of this field right?

Thank you

entry.title: Network range for Private Cloud
entry.content: Hi , our customer wants to know if its possible in private cloud to deploy on a /23 network range instead a /22 . We know that on customer onboarding forms it request a /22 network, but customer thinks this range is to wide for them and want to restrict it to /23. Is this possible on rise model or is it an requirement ?

entry.title: Basics of sustainability solution SAP Product and REACH Compliance
entry.content:
Introduction

The present blog

This blog will give you a first overview to the functionality of SAP Product and REACH Compliance. This solution is available on SAP ECC as AddOn or technically embedded in SAP S/4HANA. Here you get a first flavor of the scope of the solution.

The author

I have been working since years in the area of sustainability supporting customers in implementing product compliance solutions in several industries.

Overview to previously written blogs:

2022: Reference customers and/or customers success stories for SAP Product and REACH Compliance 

2021: How to uninstall SAP Product and REACH Compliance 2.0 (to get ready for SAP S/4HANA) 

2020: Are you affected by SCIP database of ECHA? Think about SAP Product Compliance for Discrete Ind... 

 

SAP Product and REACH Compliance

Customers

In general, all companies that produce discrete items (articles) might benefit from this solution. Automotive customers can interface the <<International Material Data System>> (IMDS) for downloading material data sheets (MDS) from their suppliers and uploading MDS to their customers.

My customers, where I have implemented SAP Product and REACH Compliance, are active in the following areas: 

  • Automotive (both OEM and Tier-1)
  • Manufacturing (flood control, pump, seal, valve, energy)
  • Hot stamping and coating technology (scope is both process and discrete)
  • Aerospace and Defense
  • Medical Engineering
  • Life Science 

Scope overview to SAP Product and REACH Compliance

The figure below introduces the scope of SAP Product and REACH Compliance. 

Regulatory List Management

Regulatory lists are mainly negative substance lists that might contain listed substances or listed substance groups. A listed substance group is for example "lead" that is being used for <<Restriction of Hazardous Substances>> (ROHS) and that contains more than 700 hundred single listed substances. Negative substance list means that the substances are either prohibited or declarable (or even both). Furthermore, the regulatory list defines for each listed substance the threshold and the algorithm for calculation.

Compliance Checks (in Compliance Requirements)

Most of the Compliance Requirements such as ROHS, IMDS, REACH-SVHC*, etc. are linked with exact one regulatory list and consume the data of them to check whether a basic material, component, product, contains these substances above threshold. This check is embedded in the compliance checks where several check criteria are in that ensure that the <<Compliance Data Object>> (CDO) is compliant or not. These compliance requirements might be linked with one default normalization that is applied for all calculations (even in automatic processing)

* REACH-SVHC: Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation, and restrictions of Chemicals - Substances of Very High Concern

Basic Material Management

For own produced components, often basic materials come into place. They can be added to own components and by this the chemical composition is known as the basic materials contain pure substances**. Using external systems like IMDS basic materials can be downloaded and used in SAP Product and REACH Compliance.

** Pure substances and listed substances are different objects in SAP Product Compliance. For seamless functionality these objects must be linked when existing on both sides.

Compliance Data

SAP Product and REACH Compliance uses its own objects, the compliance data objects. These CDOs are the counterparts of the products, components, supplier-material information, etc. in logistics. Using the Bill-of-Material-Transfer (BOM-Transfer) the system transfers the relevant objects for compliance to product compliance. Where required, high sophisticated logic to retrieve the best variant of a BOM (the best plant using the best usage and alternative) can be applied. These compliance data objects are used for compliance calculation and might have several versions (to still have access to historical data) 

Compliance Declarations from suppliers

Several formats are supported out of the box as "manual data maintenance", IPC1752A (this is a standard from IPC <<Association Connecting Electronics Industries>>), and MDS from IMDS.

Compliance Declarations for customer

Out of the box the system can create MDS to IMDS, IPC1752A format C, and Adobe Interactive Forms documents such as <<Declaration of Substances>> (DoS) and <<Certificate of Compliance>> (CoC).

Compliance Reporting

Here the system offers <<Core Data Services>> (CDS) views to retrieve data using compliance analysis from the system. Besides that, Excel templates are available to retrieve data from selected products or components and transfer them to Excel.

SAP Product and REACH Compliance - scope overview

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Deeper insight to BOM Transfer

As we have already touched above, the BOM Transfer copies the relevant objects from logistics to product compliance. In case the objects do not yet exist, they are created, otherwise the system updates them.

Objects

  • Materials (system will identify them and categorize them as "package", "bulk", or "product or product part"
  • BOM Structure (system uses specification database to store this information)
  • Supplier-material objects (these supplier parts are identified for most cases via source list or purchasing info record)

BOM Transfer triggers

As the figure below shows, there are certain triggers possible to initiate this transfer.

Manual transfer

Manually started by the user. That is what SAP has foreseen for trading goods. It might help during cutover, too. But for most use cases the customers want to have a higher automatization.

Bill of Material gets created or changed

That is also the SAP standard approach, however; the used workflows are not appreciated by customers and often a BOM gets changes to a relevant status before the material gets a relevant status. So, most customers rely on our own worklist that covers several use cases.

Supplier material links have changed

This is an appreciated SAP standard approach. Once the source list or purchasing info record gets created or deleted, the single material transfer is initiated by the system.

Material has changed

Not part of SAP standard functionality. But while applying the functionality of worklist from above we can handle this.

BOM Transfer - triggers

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Applying filters for BOM Transfer

To emphasize the powerfulness of BOM Transfer we shed some light to the filter criteria. Using two existing structures, for the materials, the BOM header, and the BOM positions filters might be configurated. By doing this we only get relevant data into product compliance. Further configuration might be used for BOM alternative handling. Last but not least, there are user exits available that allow us to realize special customer requirements, e.g., to not overwrite weight data in case a manual flag is set (weight data is always a challenge...)

Degrees of freedom using BOM Transfer

There exist two poles.

Pole 1 is to transfer each and every alternative into product compliance. This ensures maximal compliance; however, leads to complexity. Some use cases do not permit this approach. 

Pole 2 is to transfer only one structure to product compliance. This might be the latest changed BOM or the BOM with the smallest alternative.

Often a realistic scenario (using different plant nodes) gets implemented as the figure below shows.

BOM Transfer - degrees of freedom

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Product Compliance structure

The product compliance structure is being displayed in the system and used by the system for the compliance calculation. 

Objects in Product Compliance structure

The figure below provides an overview of (some of) the objects being used.

Product Compliance Structure - objects

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Normalizations

Normalizations provide a special view to the product compliance structure. So you might see all leaf elements, only basic materials, elements with issues only, etc. then normalizations come into the game. 

Beyond that there exists plenty of uses cases where normalizations get applied to manipulate the product compliance structure. The figure below shows one use case: the system creates for a procured component supplier parts. For these three suppliers you have instructed them to use exact basic material for steel XYZ. The suppliers won't give any compliance information to you. You plug-in the basic material to the component. Now the system considers both the basic material and the supplier parts and states "missing data". With the "prefer basic material" normalization, the supplier parts get suppressed in case there exists a basic material.

Product Compliance Structure - normalizations

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Summary

SAP Product and REACH Compliance is a powerful tool. For my customers I have created process documentations with 200+ pages, for sure this cannot be added into a blog. However, the basic concept on a high level is given.

In case of questions or required help just contact the author.

entry.title: Re: Sold subscription product on invoice issued from S4ha...
entry.content:
Dear MD33_SAP,

thank you for your input! However it seems that in our tenant, the CDS view you mention doesn't contain the field SoldProduct (I only found BillingPeriodOfPerfStartDate and BillingPeriodOfPerfEndDate), do you know why this could be? do we have to take any action to expose/import the field?

Good to know that the fields will be available in standard form with 2502!
Can you please share the source of this improvement? I canot find anything mentioning this in either S4hana pc or Subscription billing roadmaps.

Thank you!!

entry.title: Re: Extend SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 with SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Hi Ankita, thanks for the feedback. If I remember correct, this is a problem of the SAP Build Preview functionality in combination with the authentication. If you deploy the app to e.g cloud foundry, it should work. Maybe you like to raise the topic via support, as I am also just a user of SAP Build.

entry.title: Re: Single Sign-on: SAP Reference Architecture for Identity Access Management
entry.content:
Hello @MSo ,

 We are using IAS as a proxy and Azure as the IdP.

In Azure, we have UPN = email address.

Based on what I know, a user can authenticate to Azure only using (1) UPN or (2) email address.

Now the question is:

What should be used for the login alias? Because the email address is modifiable, is it optimal to use it for the login alias?

Can the employee ID be used for the Subject Name Identifier?

What would you recommend here?

@Slupczynski  Thank you very much  !!!

 

Many Thanks

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Cloud identity service SCIM api to retrieve personal...
entry.content: Thanks a lot! What you said is true. But consider a specific use case: for instance, all users that I manage via a Cloud Identity Service can log into an application to see a list of books. However, I want to create an API call to return personal information. What is the best way to implement this functionality?

entry.title: Transportation Charge Calculation - Absolute & Relative behavior
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We have a requirement from our customer related to Transportation Charge Calculation.

From our understanding, the scale based on quantity are responsible for handling relative (x*value = result) and absolute (if x value then determine result) behavior.

Our customer would like to mix in the same rate table absolute and relative values. We figured out that it is possible but only based on the same scale interval item.

Our customer would need the following behavior:

Have you already faced such a business requirement ? If so would you kindly share your feedback? 

Thank you so much for any feedback.

Best regards,

entry.title: List report is not getting refreshed after delete operation?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have developed a Fiori elements List report application using a SAP RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) with the managed scenario. I’m encountering an issue where, after selecting and deleting entries, the page does not automatically refresh. Instead, it leaves a blank space where the deleted records were.

Is there a solution to ensure the page refreshes automatically and the blank space is cleared after deletion?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales and Service Cloud 2502 Release Briefing
entry.content:
Hi Emmanuel,

do you have more details on slide 41: Ticket Management - Visit is now created automatically when Appointment is created

This is dangerous potentially!

entry.title: Re: CAP Application 404 error after CF deployment
entry.content:
@paulo_noronha2 

In order to access the deployed service, the URL should be like: 

.../odata/v4/processor

Therefore, the 404 error from the destination is not an issue. The service may not be accessible from Work Zone due to the xs-app.json setting. Because the two routes have the same source, the Approuter cannot distinguish them. Try the following settings:

 

{
  "source": "^/odata/(.*)$",
  "target": "/odata/$1",
  "destination": "srv-api",
  ...,
},
{
  "source": "^(.*)$",
  "target": "$1",
  "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
  ...
}

entry.title: define root view entity
entry.content: define root view entity ZCAL_I_HOLIDAY_XXX please give more information about this how can i define view .

entry.title: MRP report for Open PO monthly wise
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

 

We already have custom T-code for MRP report which includes 

Plant

FGEX Total Mat. Count

FGEX Material

Packing Material

Pack Mat. Desc.

Prod. Allocation

Status

Status Valid Date

Week

Unrestricted

In qual. Insp.

Stock

Req. Qty.

Qty. to be Proc.

Open PO Qty.

Open PO Qty.From Previous months

Open PR Qty.

Diff.

I need to include Open PO for monthly wise instead of Open PO Qty.From Previous months in total.


can anyone suggest how to do this its urgent ?

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP: Update Child entity from Parent entity with...
entry.content:
@lars_kr 

The key-%tky is the parent key. First, you need to retrieve the child entities associated with the parent and update them using their respective keys.

entry.title: Re: SELECT SINGLE vs. SELECT UP TO 1 ROWS
entry.content:
How does the variant

 

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dbtab
  UP TO 1 ROWS
    WHERE (cond).

 

fit into all this? Would it be a good compromise between avoiding unnecessary data transfer from the db and limiting the number of rows that the COUNT(*) has to read before returning the result?

entry.title: Re: Released RAP BO/CDS views/class for reading and updat...
entry.content:
@rammel_sapdev 

The BAPIs you are using are listed as Classic APIs in the Cloudification Repository Viewer.

https://sap.github.io/abap-atc-cr-cv-s4hc/?version=objectClassifications.json&q=BAPI_OBJCL_

The files objectClassifications.json or objectClassifications.csv contains the classic APIs to be consumed in your tier 2 implementations of the 3-tier extensibility model.

That means you can safely use them in Tier 2 wrapper objects.

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 4
entry.content:
Hi Matthias,

Thank you for your comprehensive examples. I'm working on a similar approach but using a standalone app with an approuter. One MTA contains the approuter and the SAPUI5 app, while another separate MTA contains your library example.
I'm struggling to make it work despite the fact that I've created a common library in the past (using a managed router).

Are any of your approaches usable with an approuter? Does this approach require additional configurations?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content: Top Document. Great explanation. Thanks for sharing such a detail content.

entry.title: Why SAP Accounting Add-ons by BlackLine Should Be Part of Every SAP Transformation
entry.content:
As enterprises continually push the pace of digital transformation to better streamline operations, SAP offers a compelling proposition for CIOs and CFOs.  Since the CFO’s approval is crucial for any investment, let’s delve into how the office of the CFO benefits from finance transformation and why SAP Accounting Add-ons by BlackLine should be integral to every ERP transformation, particularly from a CFO's perspective. World class organizations are benefitting today from taking this “gold standard” approach to both ERP transformation and financial management.

Benefits of ERP Transformation to SAP S/4HANA Cloud the CFO

Moving from legacy ERP systems to the latest version of SAP S/4HANA Cloud brings many benefits to an organization. The combination of hardware and software evolution over the years, mobile access, and cloud computing with frequent, non-disruptive access to innovation – these benefits are clear to CIOs. But there are specific benefits to for financial management organizations as well:

 

Real-Time Profitability Insights

Access profitability data by product, business line, or customer in real-time, eliminating the need for lengthy reconciliations. This empowers finance teams to make informed operational decisions swiftly.

Analytics Everywhere

Real-time analytics, out-of-the-box dashboards and KPIs ensure that all teams have up-to-date status information, enhancing customer interactions and decision-making processes. There’s no more need to steer finance operations teams with data that’s been adjusted/re-modeled/cached in a data warehouse.

Streamlined Financial Close

Integrated S/4HANA for Group Reporting aligns group and local accounting processes, ensuring consistent data and facilitating continuous accounting. With predictive accounting, you can get a view on your business performance earlier than ever.

Enhanced Cash and Liquidity Management

Tools like the Taulia portfolio provide CFOs with precise liquidity insights and then actionable processes to optimize working capital, crucial for navigating inflation and market volatility.

 

Addressing CFO Concerns About the Cloud ERP Transformation

ERP and Finance transformations can place significant burdens on IT and Finance organizations – on top of their usual work. CFOs worry about the time constraints on their core accounting teams, fearing that standard financial reporting tasks will hinder transformation efforts, especially the time spent on recurring reconciliations between subledgers and external systems, and the sign-offs that ensure reporting is complete and accurate. And since all transactions touch accounting in the end, accounting must be a part of all transition discussions. CFOs may have gone through cost-cutting or employee attrition in the past couple of years and worry about being understaffed to deal with the complexity.

Additionally, concerns about process remodeling and implementation complexity can deter progress. They may not be aware of tooling like SAP Signavio that can highlight process improvements that result from adopting best practice processes.

What’s in the back of the CFO’s mind is that the transformation initiative will be a start-and-stop process. Everything related to streamlining and transformation will grind to a halt for a significant amount of time each month while they do all the manual tasks needed to get the books closed. This is shown in the diagram below, where the blue line indicates the finance team’s focus on the transformation initiative during the month. For the period of the close, the team focuses on data cleansing and manual reconciliation work, (as shown by the black dashed line), and transformational tasks are left by the wayside.

Caption: Transformation initiative "paused" during the financial close

After the close is complete, the finance team will have to re-start the initiative – probably losing some speed and traction. And this cycle repeats each period.

However, automation and best practices – taking a "Gold Standard" approach to finance transformation - can alleviate these challenges, allowing finance teams to maintain momentum without disrupting innovation. Best-practice finance teams – teams that prioritize automation and have invested in automation enablers -  have found a way to balance out their workload during the month. This is represented by the gold line in the graphic below.

This means finance teams can re-deploy their internal resources and keep the transformation initiative moving along. The financial close still takes place, but it does not grind the streamlining engine to a halt. This is why the SAP accounting add-ons by BlackLine are a perfect complement to transformation –  part of a "Gold Standard" –  and should be adopted as early as possible in the planning cycle for a transformation.

Caption: Transformation initiative progress using the “Gold Standard” approach

 

There’s no reason to wait: it’s a “No regret” investment

Companies that have implemented SAP accounting add-ons by BlackLine on SAP ECC systems benefit from automation and enhanced control, especially in multi-system landscapes. For example, companies can implement a standardized approach for the creation of manual journal entries with strict rules for documentation and approvals, and this new “user experience” will be used for all backend ERP systems – SAP or non-SAP systems. And when the time comes to move for the finance transformation, the accountant will stay productive since the SAP accounting add-ons are already compatible.

There’s no reason to wait: it’s a smart move ahead of a major transformation

It’s highly motivating to start a large transformation initiative with a quick-win that brings the CFO and the CIO together. Hundreds of SAP customers are using SAP accounting add-ons by BlackLine and report:

Quick Time to Value

With rapid implementations in as little as 6 weeks, there is a short payback period. On average, businesses recoup their investment in an average of 9-12 months. This is useful in building the momentum for the broader SAP S/4HANA Cloud transition.

Low TCO/High ROI

The industry analyst, Nucleus Research has found that there’s a $2.77 return for every dollar spent on SAP accounting add-ons by BlackLine. While this appeals to every CFO, the CFO also benefits from having up to 50% of F&A resources freed. Accountants are empowered and own the business processes, with minimal IT support.

Embedded Leading Practices

With these best-practice processes, companies can automate many repetitive financial close activities, while enhancing balance sheet and financial statement integrity. Accountants can manage controls and changes seamlessly during the transformation.

There’s no reason to wait: companies are benefiting today!

Companies like Hershey’s and Liberty have successfully adopted this approach of adopting SAP accounting add-ons by BlackLine ahead of their SAP S/4HANA transformations, ensuring a smooth transition while empowering their finance teams.

Incorporating SAP accounting add-ons by BlackLine into your finance transformation not only streamlines financial processes but also positions your organization for sustained innovation and growth. Seamlessly integrated with SAP solutions for financial close automation, specifically SAP Advanced Financial Closing  andSAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting, these solutions are the foundation of the “Gold Standard” approach for teams to take when they move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud as a part of their finance transformation.

 

Do you want your project to benefit from the "Gold Standard" today?  You can explore accounting and financial close solutions from SAP today.

entry.title: Re: how to get count of a name that is appearing multiple times in a column
entry.content: @JBARLOW

entry.title: Re: Is it planned to have SAP OData v4 supporting Deep Update?
entry.content:
Dear Mr. Fischer,

i can't find it on the roadmap and from my tests it is still not possible to update a collection of entities or more simple "update a deep root entity".

When or if will this be possible?

Kind regards
Max

entry.title: oData V4 - delte filter attributes
entry.content:
Hello,
We using oData V4 and Fiorielements to create our application.

We have one special fitler attribute in the main entity that is not in the table. 

With the mehod

/IWBEP/IF_V4_REQU_ADV_LIST->GET_FILTER_OSQL_WHERE_CLAUSE()

it is possible to create a where clause in the backend.

Is it also possible to ignore some attributes? Or is it possible to delete special filter attributes in the request?

entry.title: Combining Lag and Snaphot KF's in one KF
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have a business requirement to merge lag and snapshot Key Figures (KFs) into a single KF. One approach is to create separate lag KFs for each lag and reference both the lag and snapshot KFs in the final KF calculation.

However, I would like to know if it is possible to achieve this without creating multiple intermediate KFs.

The requirement is as follows:

  • If the period is less than the current period minus 1, then show lag 1.
  • Otherwise, show snapshot 2.

Can anyone provide a formula for the KF calculation that meets these requirements?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Regards,

Nikhil

entry.title: Setting Up a Private SAP Advanced Event Mesh Cluster on SUSE Rancher RKE2
entry.content:
The SAP Advanced Event Mesh is a powerful tool for event-driven architecture, providing reliable, high-performance messaging across your enterprise. This blog will guide you through setting up a private SAP Advanced Event Mesh cluster using the SAP documentation as a foundation and leaving room for additional customizations, including Kubernetes setup with Rancher and screenshots for a comprehensive walkthrough.

Why Choose a Private SAP Advanced Event Mesh Cluster?

A private SAP Advanced Event Mesh cluster allows for enhanced security, control, and performance, especially critical for organizations with strict compliance or latency requirements. By deploying a private cluster, you ensure that all messaging remains within your specified infrastructure, tailored to your needs.

 

Prerequisites

Before diving into the setup process, ensure you have the following:

  1. Access to SAP Advanced Event Mesh: Ensure your subscription supports private cluster deployment, all details can be found in the SAP Discovery Center.
  2. Kubernetes Environment: Prepare a Kubernetes environment using a tool like Rancher, as baseline use the Sizing guide. For my current setup I have decided for the Standard Sizing not using a HA setup. Kindly also check the supported infrastructure providers.
  3. Network: Prepare your network setup according to the Connectivitiy Guide.

 

Step 1: Request Private region deployment

According to the SAP documentation, a private cluster deployment involves provisioning resources within your private infrastructure. It requires integrating SAP/Solace admin services to enable this. Therefore  click Request New Datacenter to open the SAP Support Portal where you can request that Datacenters be added to your private regions. Be sure to include the Subaccount ID of your advanced event mesh account in the request.

Step 2: Understanding and document the Setup Requirements

This questionnaire is specific to deployments of SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh in Customer-Controlled Regions. For questions related to deployments for Dedicated Regions, see Questionnaire: Deploying in a Dedicated Region.

To begin your planning, we have produced a questionnaire to help identify critical information required for a successful deployment, including:

  • questions common to deployments in all Kubernetes implementations, including queries about your cluster, Operational Connectivity, Messaging Connectivity, and feature requirements.
  • questions specific to the implementation of Kubernetes you have chosen for your Customer-Controlled Region, including queries about your cluster, Messaging Connectivity, and storage.

The answers to these questions help SAP determine how to configure the Mission Control Agent to create event broker services in your cluster.

Step 3: Review Private Region setup in SAP AEM

When Datacenters exist in your private regions, the Private Regions tab displays information about your private regions and the Datacenters they contain.

From here you can access the SAP AEM credentials and configurations you will need to connect your Kubernetes environment.

Downloading Image Pull Secret:

You can use the contents of either of these files as an authorization token to access the Solace Container Registry (gcr.io) where SAP's container images are stored. For more about deploying Mission Control Agent into your Kubernetes cluster, see Kubernetes Cluster Installation.

Deploying the Secret will establish the trust between your Kubernetes environment and the Solace Container Registry. 

The setup in SUSE Rancher RKE2 I will show below.

Downloading HELM Values:

The values.yaml file for the Datacenter is downloaded securely to your local storage drive via TLS. You can use the values.yaml file to deploy the Mission Control Agent into your Kubernetes cluster, see Kubernetes Cluster Installation.

The setup in SUSE Rancher RKE2 I will show below.

Downloading the Validation Tool Manifest:

You can download the validation tool manifest that is required by the cluster validation tool when validating your Kubernetes cluster with help from SAP.

The execution in SUSE Rancher RKE2 I will show below.

 

Step 4: Basic Setup using SUSE Rancher RKE2

Before you start with your Kubernetes setup check the following prerequisites / recommendations:

  • Outbound Connectivity - check if infrastructure allow port access to 55443 (Connectivitiy Guide)
  • Decide for correct Storage class - with file system xfs
  • Usage of loadbalancer controller in level L2/L4 ( not ingress L7)

First of course, check if the technical resources ( CPU, memory)  in your landscape are sufficient to deploy the content. 

Prepare Storage

For our setup we have decided and configured longhorn as Storage Class. Please be aware that you have to change the HELM Values file accordingly, before execution at the section " storageClass: longhorn".

Prepare Loadbalancer

For our setup we have decided and configured MetalLB as loadbalancer. Please be aware that you have to change the HELM Values file accordingly, before execution at the section "serviceType: LoadBalancer".

For the installation you can follow the installation guide: https://metallb.io/installation/

Once installed you still have to configure the IP or IP range which could be used by the loadbalancer: https://metallb.io/configuration/#layer-2-configuration.

Now, as the prerequisites / recommendations are fulfilled, we can go ahead with the SAP AEM Broker configuration and deployment.

Step 5: SAP AEM Broker configuration and deployment.

Upload Image Pull Secret

To establish trust you simply have to upload the pull-secret. Navigate in SUSE Rancher to Import YAML and click save.

 

To check im the import was successful you can navigate to Storage > Secrets. In this screen section you also could create trust (2nd option) if not done via Import YAML function.

Deploy Mission Control agent 

As we have now Establish trust it is time to load the images. The most common way is to execute the Kubectl shell console. Navigate in SUSE Rancher to open Kubectl shell ( consider the correct storageClass  and serviceType, see step 4).

Extract from the command line with the HELM Values file and your <values> you are deploying the mission control agent.

helm repo add solace-cloud https://cloud-agent-helm.s3.amazonaws.com

"solace-cloud" has been added to your repositories

> helm repo update

Hang tight while we grab the latest from your chart repositories...

...Successfully got an update from the "solace-cloud" chart repository

Update Complete. ⎈Happy Helming!⎈

> helm install mission-control-agent solace-cloud/solace-cloud-ca -f <values>

 

In case the deployment was successful you receive similar logs. 

NAME: mission-control-agent

LAST DEPLOYED: Thu Jan 16 16:26:56 2025

NAMESPACE: default

STATUS: deployed

REVISION: 1

TEST SUITE: None

NOTES:

Thank you for installing solace-cloud-ca.

Your release is named mission-control-agent.

To learn more about the release, try:

  $ helm status mission-control-agent

  $ helm get all mission-control-agent

Datacenter ID is: your ID

Datacenter region is not set.

Diagnostics is disabled.

Not using service class selectors.

Not using region selector.

Not using zone selectors.

Using storage class: longhorn

 

Deploy SAP AEM Private Broker 

Access your public SAP AEM Broker Management console, Cluster Manager > Create Service, provide a meaningful name and select the Cloud type "Private Cloud" and the Region of your private datacenter. (this private datacenter has been register by the mission control agent in the project step). Slect the version and service type you would like to work with and "Create Broker".

The broker setup will take some minutes similar to the cloud broker setup. In parallel check the logs of your Kubernetes cluster.

If the setup was successful you can access the broker through the cloud console and use the standard features like Manage, Monitor, Configuration and TryMe.

HappyPrivateEventing

entry.title: How to get access to DEV environment login details with the company ID
entry.content: Hello Team, i am having issues getting into DEV environment, i have the company ID but can't seem to get the log in details. Any suggestions please

entry.title: Upload files to specific Folders inside SAP DMS from SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi

We are currently exploring to use DMS for our Process Automation Scenarios. We want to enable the end users to upload files to DMS using SBPA Forms. There are many Automation process, so we want the files to get stored inside respective project folders. 

We observed when a end user uploads a file thru SBPA Forms, a new folder is created each time. However we would like to get the files stored inside a particular folder of DMS. E.g. Sales Order Automation files will go to Sales Order Folders, Invoice will go to Invoice folder and so on. All these folders will reside under the root of the repository which we created in DMS.

Is this possible to do? So far, we have not found a way. 

Any pointers are helpfull. 

Regards

Sandip

entry.title: Integration SAP PPM 6.1 with S/4HANA 2023 FP2
entry.content:
Hi,

My customer is starting the conversion from SAP ECC to S/4HANA 2023 FP2 Private Cloud Edition (RISE). They are running SAP PPM 6.1 integrated with their current SAP ECC system. First, they wanted to set up EPPM (would be greenfield) in S/4 during the conversion project. But now, they decided to do that later after S/4 Go live. Are there any specifics to be taken into account for the integration of their current on-premise SAP PPM version and S/4HANA 2023 FP2 Private Cloud Edition?

Many thanks for insights! Goedele

entry.title: Re: Manage Customer Hierarchies - Import customer Hierarchy
entry.content: Can you share the steps to raise a feature request?

entry.title: does not appear subscriptions menu "Asset performance management" in BTP sub-account
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to use T&D of SAP Asset Performance Management on cloud foundry. i did select the Instances and subscription on the navigation, and at the top right in subaccount select create. however  subscriptions menu "Asset performance management" does not appear. Please see a screenshot below.

Please let me know which settings I should check.
#APM, #BTP, #TTD

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration – Remote OData API’s for Integration Flows
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, Can Anyone help me in this.

I am trying to create one iflow in my sap integration suite tanent from postman by consuming below api from api.sap.com

before creating a iflow i am trying to create X-CSRF Token for that i am using the below steps.

I have set the header for fetching the X-CSRF token.

I am getting 200 ok but in my headers i am not able to get X-CSRF token in my header section.

this is the header after sending the request.

Please help me in this. It's showing unauthorized while i am posting or consuming any of the api.

I am using Trial Account.

Thanks 
Dhruv

entry.content: Hello Saravanan. We are currently facing the exact same problem as described on this web page. What was the conclusion?

entry.title: Re: Chart Display Issues with SAP BW Live Queries in SAC
entry.content: Hi @chmbilal, thanks for your input! Could you provide more details or an example on how to achieve this with scripting in SAC?

entry.title: Re: Project Settlement in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Just seen your very helpful blog.

If the project is settled monthly and various invoices with different currencies are included in the settlement, what is the FX activity - that is does it take the date of settlement or the date of the invoices for FX valuations?

entry.title: Re: Delivered quantity is showing short in Production Order
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Bill To selection in non-po invoices
entry.content:
We have implemented an endpoint in Ariba Business Network to allow suppliers to select it when creating a credit memo or a non-PO invoice. However, during testing in the Test environment, the endpoint does not appear in the dropdown list of the "Bill to" field for suppliers.

The endpoint is correctly configured and active in CIG. What additional configuration or step is required to ensure it appears in the suppliers' selection?

entry.title: Re: Delivered quantity is showing short in Production Order
entry.content: is this report also working if you have a different production unit in the order? Best regards, Fabian

entry.title: Re: Migrating SAP EH&S DMS Documents in databse to content server
entry.content: @ pugazendhi_pugazendhi : we are also having the same requirement, could you please update how did you resolve it

entry.title: Integration between Cloud ALM and non-SAP architectures like Salesforce
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Would anyone know if there's the possibility of integrating Cloud ALM with other deployment tools from non-SAP world, like Copado for Salesforce, etc.?

Similar to what Cloud ALM is doing with CTMS/ATO...

Thank you,
Bruno

entry.title: Re: Issue with DataHub Deployment: PayerID Not Displaying in Production
entry.content: Thanks for your suggestion. There is the same branch deployed for DEV, UAT, and Production, and I am sending the same IDoc for all environments. However, while it works perfectly fine in lower environments like DEV and UAT, it encounters issues in Production. If, as you suggest, the issue is related to loading extensions, could you help clarify why it still works fine in DEV and UAT but fails in Production? Additionally, if you suspect that extension overriding might be the cause, could you suggest how and where to check the logs for extensions to verify this? Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Loading/Creating Story Reports in Non-Prod...
entry.content: Hi Oscar, did you manage to find a solution? We are facing the same issue. We have raised a ticket to SAP so it would be interesting to see if your issue was solved and how. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: OpenText VIM: Invoice Approval Process and Chart of Authority
entry.content:
Hi,

I have received new requirement, please see below and guide me how to proceed along with configuration steps. This is very urgent requirement, kindly do the needful

As of now process is below:

  • PDF Aftermarket Invoices are read and entered manually into MIRO by an office agent against po with relevant GR’s
  • VIM is used to process IGS invoices only. PDF Invoices are scanned by OCR and data is verified by office agent and then process against Non product PO or Accounting Allocation (without PO) and sent for approval

Process to be is:

  • Starting Jan.2025, we must be able to receive AP e-invoices in our SAP (for Aftermarket invoices) VIM (for IGS invoices requiring the workflow approval VIM offers)  systems and process automatically the e-invoices information as we won't be able to continue existing process of manually inputting the invoice given the xml format.
  • We have a 2 year period where we could still receive PDF copies and the e-invoices, but only if the supplier agrees to do so, as he is not legally forced to do so. 
  • As of January 2025, we cannot reject an e-invoice if a supplier decides to use the e-invoicing format

Regards,

Deepthi

entry.title: Re: How to modify Delivered Quantity field
entry.content: Note that the field Delivered Quantity is linked to LFIMG_FLO (and not LFIMG) in the default values of VL01n

entry.title: SO in SAP B1 - 'Cannot select serial numbers; fractional quantity exists in row 10' error
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I am creating a Sales order and its showing me the error 'Cannot select serial numbers; fractional quantity exists in row 10'.

The item in row 10 is a newly created item, I followed the same procedure as all the other created items, but this is the first time I have an error related to fractional quantity. 

Hope anyone can help me understand what is this issue and how to solve it.

entry.title: Hi Everyone
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have over three years of experience working with SAP, primarily focusing on SAP Ariba, while continuously expanding my knowledge in other areas. SAP is evolving at a rapid pace, integrating new technologies and constantly adapting to market trends. Keeping up with these changes is challenging, which is why being part of a strong and collaborative community is essential for professional growth.

I am excited to share my experience and, at the same time, learn from all of you. I look forward to exchanging knowledge and contributing to the development of this community!
